## [00:00 - 03:00]

Okay, let's talk about scarcity and evergreen launches. And I think when I talk about evergreen, this is the number one question I get. The people have the most questions about scarcity. So one way is to not have any types of scarcity. You just have your three pieces of pre-launch content and then you open cart and then you just hope people buy. The key word there is hope. This is how you cut your results in half. If you wanna only get half the results from your evergreen, then just don't have scarcity. And you'll get half the results. And I know a really, just a huge big name thought leader, I absolutely guarantee you know this person, you've heard of this person and they did this. And I'm like, what are you doing? And I don't know why they did it that way, but when they switched it around, they started to have real scarcity, then all of a sudden the results doubled. And this is just classic. We see this over and over and over. That's how launches work. There's an open cart and a closed cart. And we've talked about the three types of scarcity. Either the offer goes away or the price goes up or bonuses go away. In some way, the offer either goes away or is diminished in quality if people don't get it at the end of the cart. So people are like, well, if it's an evergreen, then how can that offer ever go away? Well, it's the same way. It's like if you get a magazine, you set up for a magazine subscription, they always have a special deal when you first start out. That special deal is just when you're starting out. It's not for when you renew. If you're signing up for internet service, they often will have a special deal. If you're signing up for sell service, they'll often have a special deal when you first start out. That's the way it is with your evergreen. So someone joins your list. And then shortly after they join your list, some period of time, depending on what type of evergreen launch you're doing, they go through your PLC. They go through your pre-launch. And then the cart opens, you send them an email, okay, we're taking orders right now. And then some period of five to seven days is your open cart. Just like in your internal launch, your JV launch, these rules all hold. Five to seven days later, you close cart. And that person, based on when they joined your list, based on their email address, that person needs to purchase by the end of their cart period. So someone joins your list, they're gonna go through your pre-launch and they're gonna have that five to seven day window. And if they buy in there, then they get the launch offer. They get the special offer. They get the special pricing. They get the special bonuses. Or they're allowed to actually buy the product before it goes off the market. It's just, it's so simple. they come into your universe, they get their launch with a special offer. Now there's a lot of different ways you can track this with software. I generally do it based on email.
